title: else
subtitle: What to do otherwise
goal: Learn to define an alternative path using else.
explanation: If the if condition returns false, the else block runs.
This lets you describe two different paths.
One path is for true, and the other is for false.
This is the most common form of branching in programming.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: If it is sunny, you go for a walk. Otherwise, you stay home. else describes the “otherwise” option.

remember: else runs when if returns false.
if and else describe two possible paths.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners sometimes think else checks a new condition. In fact, else simply runs when the preceding condition did not pass.
